_meta.purpose: Reference diagnoses for PR E judge prompts — one valid answer shape per seeded anomaly. The judge scores candidate diagnoses against the 5-criterion rubric in eval/judges/diagnosis-judge.md, NOT against literal match to these references. These reference answers are 'one valid shape' so the judge has something to anchor 'right hypothesis' against.

olist_horizon.note: Week 1 = (START_TS .. START_TS+7d). Week 26 ends at END_TS = 2026-06-01 00:00 UTC.

sp-revenue-drop-w4.anomaly_summary: Sao Paulo (SP) state revenue dropped ~30% in week 4 of the dataset (~late Dec 2025) vs the surrounding 12-week baseline.
investigation_should_examine: Which product categories drove the drop (was it a single category collapsing or broad across SP)
Whether payment types shifted (e.g., voucher to credit_card substitution) in SP that week
Whether delivery / cancellation / fulfillment rates correlate with the drop
Whether the drop concentrates in specific SP cities (sao paulo, campinas, santos) or is state-wide
key_evidence_signals: related_segments should show whether other states moved correlated (broad demand shock) or whether SP is isolated (local cause)
Time series should show a single week dip, not a sustained slide

electronics-spike-w2.anomaly_summary: Electronics category order count spiked ~2.5x in week 2 of the dataset (~mid Dec 2025) vs the 12-week baseline.
investigation_should_examine: Whether the spike is driven by a few high-volume customers (concentrated) vs distributed across many
Geographic concentration of the spike (single state vs broad)
Whether revenue scaled with order_count or whether average order value collapsed (margin vs vanity metric)
Possible promotional, seasonal, inventory-clearance, or single-large-buyer factors
key_evidence_signals: related_segments should show whether other categories moved together (broad seasonality) or whether electronics is isolated
Revenue scaling with order count rules out an AOV-driven artefact

voucher-dropoff-w10-on.anomaly_summary: Voucher payment method volume dropped to near-zero (~5% of baseline) starting week 10 (~Feb 2026) and persisted through the end of the data horizon.
investigation_should_examine: Whether other payment methods absorbed the voucher volume (substitution toward credit_card / boleto / debit_card)
Whether the dropoff is recovering or sustained through the end of the horizon
Whether the dropoff correlates with specific states / cities / order types (broad system change vs targeted)
Whether this looks like a system / process / program change (e.g., voucher program ended) vs natural decay
key_evidence_signals: Time series of payment_value by payment_type should show vouchers cliff while other methods continue (substitution pattern)
The sustained shape (weeks 10-26) rules out single-week incident; suggests system/program change
